name: list_processes
description:
Checks whether specific services are currently running on the local machine — by process name or port. Returns running status, PID, and port for Docker, Redis, Postgres, Node, and any named service.
Use when: you need to verify a dev server, database, or container runtime is up before running commands or tests.
Scope: local machine only. Reads /proc or uses pgrep/lsof — no network calls.
Not for: reading log output (use tail_system_logs), starting or stopping services, or checking remote hosts.

services.description: Services to check. Well-known names: docker, dockerd, redis, postgres, mysql, mongodb, nginx, node, python, uvicorn, celery, flower, rabbitmq, git, ssh-agent, gpg-agent, nodemon, watchman, vite, webpack, esbuild, pytest, jest, vitest, eslint, prettier, prometheus, grafana, jaeger, zipkin, opentelemetry-collector, ngrok, cloudflared, cron, vault. Also accepts custom process names or 'port:3000' to check by port. Default: all well-known services.

include_ports.description: If true, also report which ports each detected process is listening on. Default: true.
